data.title: Adecoagro launches notes offering
data.description:
Adecoagro (NYSE:AGRO) has announced a private offering of senior unsecured notes. The timing of pricing and terms of the notes are subject to market conditions and other factors.

The proceeds from the notes offering will be used by the company (i) to fund a tender offer conducted by the company to purchase for cash any and all of its 6.000% Notes due 2027, and (ii) the remainder, if any, for general corporate purposes, including capital expenditures and liability management.

data.title: Bank First to acquire Centre 1 Bancorp in all-stock deal valued at ~$174.3M
data.description:
Bank First (NASDAQ:BFC) is set to acquire 100% shares of Centre 1 Bancorp in an all-stock transaction valued at ~$174.3M.

Shares were -2.05% to $123.20.

Centre 1 Bancorp is the holding company for the nationally chartered bank, The First National Bank and Trust Company.

Centre shareholders will have the right to receive 0.9200 of Bank First's common stock in exchange for each share they own.

The boards of both companies have approved the proposed merger.

The combined company is estimated to have assets of ~$5.91B, loans of ~$4.58B, and deposits of ~$4.89B.

The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Dell's EPS might double by 2030 due to enterprise AI adoption: BofA
data.description:
Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL), along with other IT hardware companies, stand to benefit over the next several years due to the growth of enterprise and sovereign artificial intelligence initiatives, according to Bank of America.

Dell shares edged up 3% during early market action on Friday. Shares have recovered significantly since hitting a two-year low in early April and have traded at year-to-date highs over the past week.

"The headwinds of the past decade from public cloud migrations (Capex to OpEx spending trends) should reverse over the next decade (bloated OpEx to ROI driving Capex) as enterprises harness AI to drive productivity," said BofA analysts, led by Wamsi Mohan, in a Friday investor note. "We expect DELL to grow its topline by +12% over the next 5 years vs +2% over the prior 5 and materially raise our ests to reflect this tailwind."

Based on an analysis of market share, pricing and operating margin assumptions, BofA's base case has Dell's earnings per share increasing to $19.01 by 2030. 

"Success in AI servers will drive lower margin rates but would also drive higher OP dollars," Mohan noted. "Given our view that the AI TAM has many years of growth ahead, we view higher OP dollars (+12.1% over the next 5 years) vs. +4.6% over the prior 5 years as a key driver for improved cash flows."

BofA reiterated its Buy rating and boosted its price objective to $165 from $155.

data.title: Stock market opens up as Wall Street's cheerful mood continues
data.description:
Wall Street opened higher on Friday as market sentiment continued to be upbeat, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed at record highs. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.3%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.87%.

Building permits and housing starts rose more than expected in June.

Wall Street closed out trading higher on Thursday, with both the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 surging to fresh record highs.

“Markets have put in a decent performance over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both reaching fresh all-time highs, with the global rally mostly continuing this morning. The advance was driven by another batch of positive U.S. data, including higher-than-expected retail sales and then a 5th consecutive weekly decline in initial jobless claims,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

With the stronger data and a continued rise in market inflation pricing, investors dialed down the amount of Fed rate cuts expected this year to 43bps, the lowest this has been since February. Reid added. 

“The problem for the Federal Reserve and markets is that the U.S. administration’s policy continues to create considerable uncertainty about the economic outlook,” UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Sentiment is forecasted up slightly to 61.4 in the first reading for July from 60.7 in the June final.

“Political partisanship will be delayed in the Michigan consumer sentiment data (Republicans live in the best of all possible worlds, Democrats live in the worst of times—according to survey evidence),” Donovan said.

data.title: Chevron closes Hess takeover after winning Exxon arbitration
data.description:
Just hours after winning its arbitration dispute with Exxon Mobil (XOM) over Hess' (HES) lucrative assets offshore Guyana, Chevron (NYSE:CVX) said Friday it closed its deal for Hess and plans to nominate John Hess, the smaller company's longtime CEO, to its board.

The Federal Trade Commission had set aside orders Thursday that had previously barred Hess from serving on Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) board.

Even as it awaited the arbitration verdict, Chevron (CVX) was making preparations so it could close the deal quickly; information technology workers from Chevron and Hess (HES) have met regularly to plan the integration, and Hess employees were told they could request a severance package following the deal's close.

The acquisition means Chevron (CVX) now owns a 30% position in Guyana's Stabroek block, which has more than 11B boe discovered recoverable resource; 463K net acres of high-quality inventory in the Bakken shale; complementary assets in the U.S. Gulf with 31K boe/day; and natural gas assets in Southeast Asia with 57K boe/day.

The combined company's capital spending budget is expected at $19B-$22B.

"This accretive transaction is expected to drive significant free cash flow and production growth into the 2030s," Chevron (CVX) CFO Eimear Bonner said. "We are quickly integrating our two companies and expect to achieve $1B in annual run-rate cost synergies by the end of 2025."

data.title: 5 stocks to watch on Friday: SCHW, AXP, MMM, TLN, and HES
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched record closes, helped by the retail sales report and earnings releases. Here are four stocks to watch out for:

Charles Schwab (SCHW) stock rose 2.3% before the opening bell after Q2 earnings and revenue beat Wall Street expectations as core net new balances grew 31%, sweep cash rose, and the company continued to reduce its bank supplemental funding. The firm reported Q2 adjusted EPS of $1.14, topping the consensus of $1.10, and net revenue of $5.85B, beating the $5.74B consensus.

American Express (AXP) shares rose 1.2% in premarket trade after the credit card lender posted stronger-than-expected Q2 earnings, helped by robust spending by customers and as the credit quality remained strong. The company also reaffirmed its full-year EPS outlook of $15.00-$15.50 vs. the average analyst estimate of $15.24.

3M (MMM) stock was up 2.6% before the market open and is set to open at its highest since September 2021, as the firm beat both its top and bottom line second-quarter estimates. The company also upped its full-year adjusted EPS outlook to  $7.75 to $8.00 vs. $7.69 consensus. The updated guidance includes impact from tariffs. The firm looks to be reaping benefits from its cost-cutting measures, and efforts to focus on high-margin products.

Talen Energy (TLN) shares surged 17.9% in premarket trade after the company said it has agreed to acquire a power plant in Pennsylvania and another in Ohio for ~$3.5B after adjusting for estimated tax benefits. The firm will issue ~$3.8B in new debt to fund the acquisitions and refinance target debt.

Hess (HES) rose 7.7% before the opening bell as Chevron received clearance to proceed with its planned $53B acquisition of Hess (HES) after an arbitration panel dismissed Exxon Mobil's (XOM) claim that it had a contractual right to bid for Hess' assets offshore Guyana. Chevron added it has closed the acquisition deal, which it had originally struck in October 2023.

data.title: Sify Technologies reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Sify Technologies press release (NASDAQ:SIFY): Q1 loss of INR 389 Million.
  • Revenues of INR 10,723 Million.
  • Shares -3.5% PM.

data.title: Ally Financial Q2 earnings rise past consensus as credit loss provision falls
data.description:
Ally Financial (NYSE:ALLY) on Friday turned in Q2 earnings and revenue that topped consensus estimates as the auto finance company lowered expenses and set aside less money for bad debt.

Q2 adjusted EPS of $0.99, exceeding the $0.81 average analyst estimate, climbed from $0.58 in the prior quarter and $0.73 a year earlier.

Q2 adjusted total net revenue of $2.06B, vs. $2.04B expected, slipped from $2.07B in Q1 and virtually unchanged from Q2 2024.

ALLY shares edged down 0.6% in premarket trading.

Adjusted provision for credit losses slid to $384M in Q2 from $497M in the prior three-month period and $457M a year ago. The Y/Y decrease was driven by the sale of Credit Card and lower retail auto net charge-offs, Ally (NYSE:ALLY) said.

Adjusted noninterest expense retreated to $1.26B in Q2 from $1.32B in Q1 and $1.29B in the year-ago quarter.

Net financing revenue (excluding core OID) was $1.53B, up from $1.49B in Q1 and $1.53B in Q2 2024.

Core return on tangible common equity accelerated to 13.6% in Q2 from 8.3% in the earlier quarter and 10.7% in 2024's Q2.

Pretax income by segment: 

  • Auto Finance: $472M vs. $375M in Q1.
  • Insurance: $28M vs. $2M in Q1.
  • Corporate Finance: $96M vs. $76M in Q1.

Meanwhile, the company revised its 2025 outlook for average earnings assets growth to -2% Y/Y from flat Y/Y. It maintained its annual guidance for net interest margin, adjusted other revenue, adjusted noninterest expense and net charge offs.

data.title: Meta declines to sign EU’s AI Code, calling it overreach
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) said it would not sign the European Commission’s Code of Practice for the EU's new set of rules governing AI.

"Europe is heading down the wrong path on AI. We have carefully reviewed the European Commission’s Code of Practice for general-purpose AI (GPAI) models and Meta won’t be signing it," said Meta’s head of global affairs Joel Kaplan in a post on LinkedIn. "This Code introduces a number of legal uncertainties for model developers, as well as measures which go far beyond the scope of the AI Act. "

Earlier this month, in a letter signed by representatives from about 45 organizations, business leaders asked the president of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, to postpone implementation of the region's AI regulation for two years because they say it puts Europe's AI ambitions at risk. Some parts of the rules are set to come into effect next month.

"We share concerns raised by these businesses that this over-reach will throttle the development and deployment of frontier AI models in Europe, and stunt European companies looking to build businesses on top of them," said Kaplan in its post.

Last week, the European Commission unveiled the GPAI Code of Practice, a voluntary tool prepared by independent experts in a multi-stakeholder process, designed to help industry comply with the AI Act’s obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models.

The framework includes copyright protections for creators and transparency requirements for advanced AI models. It also requires developers to provide documentation to describe the features of their AI models.

In May 2024, the Council of the European Union gave final approval to the tentative agreement endorsed by EU lawmakers on rules governing AI. The Council had said that the so-called artificial intelligence act follows a 'risk-based' approach, which means the higher the risk to cause harm to society, the stricter the rules.
